sutta: the buddha said monks these 5 ancient brahmin things are now seen in dogs but not in brahmins what 5 monks in former times brahmins approach only a brahmani never a non brahmani now they go to brahmani and non brahmani alike monks today dogs go to dogs only never to other creatures this month is the first ancient brahmin thing now seen in dogs but not in brahmins monks in former times brahmins approached the brahmani or brahmini only in season and never at a time at other times now they go to a brahmani both in and out of season monks today dogs go to dogs only in season never at other times this monks is the second ancient brahmin thing now seen in dogs not in brahmins monks in former times brahmins neither bought nor sold a brahmani but consorting just where affection was mutual fostered concord now they do any of these things monks today dogs neither bind or sell dogs but consult just where affection is mutual and foster concord this month is the third ancient brahmin thing now seen in dogs not in brahmins monks in former times brahmins ordered neither treasure grain silver nor gold now they do these things monks today dogs hot neither treasure grain silver nor gold this monks is the fourth ancient brahmin thing now seen in dogs not in brahmins monks in former times brahman sought food for the evening meal in the evening for the morning meal in the morning now after cramming their bellies to the utmost they take away the remainder monks today dogs seek food for the evening meal in the evening for the morning meal in the morning this mouse is the fifth ancient brahmin thing now seen in dogs not in brahmins barely monks these are the 5 ancient brahmin things as the end of the suta
commentary: this is quite an unusual suta where the buddha is investigating the brahmins is comparing them to dogs and saying that dogs are even better than these brahmins these brahmins originally were supposed to be priests like ascetics and they were supposed to after their certain years of training and they're supposed to be like a renuncian where they beg for their food and all that and if they don't want to continue their that life then if they get married they only get married to brahmani that means 1 of the brahmin cast also a female of the brahmin cast and they only like sleep only when the woman is in season and just to get a child and not for enjoyment but now this buddha is saying comparing them to dogs the first 1 that in ancient times brahmins only approach brahmani now they approach brahmani and the non-brahmani other cars also whereas dogs only go to dogs to procreate and they still do and the second 1 is that brahmin only approached brahmani during her season not at other times just as but but now they they do it any time they 1 whereas dogs still keep the ancient tradition of going to the beach only in season not at other times and then brahmins formerly neither bought nor sold money but sort of got married just where the affection was mutual and nowadays they buy and sell money whereas dogs still don't do that and then uh in ancient times um brahmins and never hoarded their their things like never hoarded treasure grain silver or gold and now they hot these things whereas dogs still don't do that and the last 1 is uh brahmins formerly used to seek for their food in the evening if they need food in the morning they'll go and look for in the morning if they need it in the evening they look for it in the evening they never store their food whereas now they eat as much as they can and then take away the remainder and store it up whereas dogs also still don't store their food so the buddha is saying that dogs are even better than brahmins and the reason you can see if you study more of the sutas is that the brahmins now were very critical of the buddha they thought themselves as the superior and they looked down on the buddha and and said that they were born from the mouth of brahma their god they were born from the mouth of brahma whereas all other caste and the other 3 cars were born from the feet of brahma and of course the other cars didn't accept it because like the buddha belonged to the warrior cast the katya or shatria casa and that was the nobles and of course they con the noble considered themselves higher than the brahmins but the brahmins uh considered themselves higher and so because they were um arch enemies sort of that's why the buddha really uh castigated them in this utah and this suit also shows that the buddha also has his own peculiar habits and his behavior and seems to show if you study the sutas that the buddha still has some of these human qualities that carried over from previous life whereas this contrary to what the commentary tries to say and the commentary tries to say that the buddha is so perfect that he has no faults whatsoever compared to the arahants are supposed to still have faults and peculiarities in their behavior and this suit is 1 of those that shows that the buddha still does calls them now the next few times

items: approach a brahmani only in season
do not buy or sell a brahmini
do not hoard treasure grain, silver, or gold
seek food for the evening meal in the evening and morning
